Ukraine Conflict News





30 December 2025 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • CENTCOM Forces Remove ISIS Operatives in Syria After Large-Scale Strike CENTCOM 30 Dec 2025 -- U.S. and partner forces killed or captured nearly 25 ISIS operatives during the days that followed a Dec. 19 large-scale strike in Syria.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Meeting with Deputy Minister of Defence Anna Tsivileva President of Russia 30 Dec 2025 -- Vladimir Putin held a working meeting with State Secretary - Deputy Minister of Defence and Chair of the Defenders of the Fatherland Foundation to support participants in the special military operation Anna Tsivileva.
  • Russian Army Most Combat-Ready in World - Russian Defense Minister Sputnik 30 Dec 2025 -- The Russian army is the most combat-ready in the world, Russian Defense Minister Andrei Belousov said on Tuesday.
  • Belarus deploys Russia's nuclear-capable hypersonic missiles amid tensions with West Press TV 30 Dec 2025 -- Belarus has started deploying Russia's nuclear-capable Oreshnik hypersonic missile system on its territory, a move Moscow says will bolster regional security and deter further Western military escalation.

  • Navy does not deny locking onto PLA warship with fire control radar CNA 30 Dec 2025 -- The Navy on Tuesday was mum over whether it locked its fire control radar onto a Chinese warship participating in a drill in waters off Taiwan's southwestern shores but did not deny employing such a maneuver.
  • Taiwan's military highlights Chinese disinformation during large-scale drill CNA 30 Dec 2025 -- Taiwan's military on Tuesday warned the public about a wave of Chinese disinformation circulating online amid Beijing's ongoing large-scale military exercise around the island, urging people not to fall for false claims aimed at undermining public morale.
  • PLA rockets land inside Taiwan's 24 nautical mile contiguous zone: MND CNA 30 Dec 2025 -- Taiwan's military on Tuesday said the People's Liberation Army (PLA) fired 27 rockets during the second day of its latest military drill near Taiwan, with 10 landing at sea within the nation's 24-nautical mile contiguous zone, making it the closest ever Chinese live-fire exercise.
  • Military conducts defense drills in New Taipei amid Chinese exercises CNA 30 Dec 2025 -- Taiwan's military on Tuesday conducted a series of emergency combat readiness drills in New Taipei in response to two days of military exercises by the Chinese People's Liberation Army around Taiwan.
  • KMT, TPP block consideration of special defense budget for 5th time CNA 30 Dec 2025 -- Kuomintang (KMT) and Taiwan People's Party (TPP) lawmakers on Tuesday blocked a bill authorizing a special defense budget of NT$1.25 trillion (US$39.8 billion) from being placed on the agenda for Friday's Legislative Yuan plenary session, the opposition's fifth such move this month.
  • 90 PLA warplanes cross Taiwan Strait median line as China stages drills CNA 30 Dec 2025 -- Ninety People's Liberation Army (PLA) warplanes were detected crossing the median line of the Taiwan Strait between 6 a.m. Monday and 6 a.m. Tuesday while China staged military exercises around Taiwan, according to the Ministry of National Defense (MND).
  • Spokesperson statement on China's military exercise around Taiwan European External Action Service (EEAS) 30 Dec 2025 -- China's recent military exercise further increases cross-strait tensions and endangers international peace and stability.
  • Statement on China's military drills: December 2025 UK FCDO 30 Dec 2025 -- The FCDO has issued a statement in response to the latest Chinese military exercises around Taiwan.
  • Justice Mission 2025: Long-range Live-fire Joint Fire Strikes PRC MND 30 Dec 2025
  • PLA Eastern Theater Command conducts drills in waters to the east of Taiwan Island on all-dimensional force projection and seizure of key ports PRC MND 30 Dec 2025
  • Fujian Coast Guard conducts law enforcement and control drills off Taiwan Island PRC MND 30 Dec 2025
  • Units of PLA Eastern Theater Command conduct long-range live fire drills to the south of Taiwan Island PRC MND 30 Dec 2025
  • Ground forces of PLA Eastern Theater Command conduct long-range live fire drills to the north of Taiwan Island PRC MND 30 Dec 2025
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 30 Dec 2025
  • CCG, GT jointly release poster 'Throat-choking'; HIMARS-carrying cargo ship depicted intercepted off Taiwan island Global Times 30 Dec 2025 -- On Tuesday, the China Coast Guard (CCG) continued to organize task forces to carry out comprehensive law enforcement patrol in the waters off the Taiwan island, the Matsu and Wuqiu islands. The CCG, in conjunction with the Global Times, released the "Throat-choking" poster, demonstrating the CCG's capability to control key maritime areas and seize dangerous targets.
  • PLA Eastern Theater Command conducts joint drills around Taiwan; 'legitimate and necessary action to safeguard sovereignty and natl unity' Global Times 30 Dec 2025 -- Multiple forces of the Eastern Theater Command of the Chinese People's Liberation Army (PLA) started drills code-named "Justice Mission 2025" around Taiwan Island on Monday, said Shi Yi, spokesperson for the theater command.
  • PLA conducts major drill near Taiwan People's Daily 30 Dec 2025 -- The People's Liberation Army conducted on Monday a large-scale, interservice exercise surrounding China's island of Taiwan, sending a clear warning to "Taiwan independence" separatists and external forces meddling in the Taiwan question.
  • Military drills target "Taiwan independence" separatist activities, foreign interference: spokesperson People's Daily 30 Dec 2025 -- A Chinese government spokesperson on Monday said the military drills conducted by the People's Liberation Army (PLA) are specifically targeted at "Taiwan independence" separatist activities and external interference.
  • Commentary: The clear message behind the PLA's latest drills around Taiwan People's Daily 30 Dec 2025 -- The Chinese People's Liberation Army (PLA) on Monday launched a series of drills code-named "Justice Mission 2025" around Taiwan Island, dispatching troops from its ground, air, naval and rocket forces to deter "Taiwan independence" separatist forces and external interference.
  • PLA Eastern Theater Command conducts drills on assault against maritime targets, anti-air and anti-submarine operations to the north and south of Taiwan Island PRC MND 30 Dec 2025

  • Illustrious Commander Ushers in Golden Age in Strengthening Revolutionary Armed Forces KCNA 30 Dec 2025 -- On December 30, 2011, the Workers' Party of Korea and the people of the DPRK held in high esteem the respected Comrade Kim Jong Un as the supreme commander of the armed forces of the DPRK.
  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Gives Field Guidance at Major Munitions Industry Enterprise KCNA 30 Dec 2025 -- Kim Jong Un , general secretary of the Workers' Party of Korea and president of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, visited a major munitions industry enterprise on Dec. 28 to learn about the production of weapons and combat equipment.

  • MoD inks Rs 4,666 crore contracts for Close Quarter Battle Carbine & Heavy Weight Torpedoes India PIB 30 Dec 2025 -- Ministry of Defence, on December 30, 2025, signed contracts worth a total cost of Rs 4,666 crore for the procurement of Close Quarter Battle (CQB) Carbine and Heavy Weight Torpedoes.

  • Boeing signs $8.6 billion contract to supply Israel with F-15 fighter jets Press TV 30 Dec 2025 -- US aviation giant Boeing has signed an $8.6 billion deal to supply Israel with advanced F-15IA fighter jets, the Pentagon said, coinciding with a meeting between US President Donald Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu in Florida.

  • Estonia Signs Contract with the First Manufacturer of the Future Defence Industry Park Estonia CDI 30 Dec 2025 -- The Estonian Centre for Defence Investments (ECDI) and the company Nitrotol OÜ have signed a contract for the production of military explosives and ammunition, including mines and charges, in the defence industry park to be established in Ermistu, Pärnu County.

  • AFP in 2025: Reduced threats, enhanced capabilities, deeper alliances Philippine News Agency 30 Dec 2025 -- The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) on Tuesday announced that it had achieved significant gains in internal security, counterterrorism, disaster response, capability development and international defense cooperation in 2025.

  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Saudi Foreign Minister Prince Faisal bin Farhan Al Saud US Dept. of State 30 Dec 2025
  • Secretary Rubio's Call with UAE Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Sheikh Abdullah bin Zayed Al Nahyan US Dept. of State 30 Dec 2025

Defense Industry

  • Contracts For Dec. 30, 2025

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

    Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Israeli court advances forced displacement of Palestinians in favor of Jewish settlers in Silwan Press TV 30 Dec 2025 -- Israel's high court has advanced the forced displacement of Palestinian families in the Batn al-Hawa neighborhood of Silwan, south of the Al-Aqsa Mosque, following rulings on two of four petitions filed by residents, according to the al-Quds Governorate.

  • UNRWA head slams 'outrageous' Israeli law to cut water, energy to agency's facilities Press TV 30 Dec 2025 -- The head of the UN agency for Palestinian refugees has strongly slammed new Israeli legislation to cut water and energy to UNRWA facilities, calling it a violation of international law and a direct challenge to the United Nations system.
  • Statement by UNICEF Regional Director for the Middle East and North Africa Edouard Beigbeder on the death of children in the Gaza Strip due to severe winter weather conditions UNICEF 30 Dec 2025 -- UNICEF is deeply saddened by the preventable tragic death of Ata Mai, a seven-year-old boy, who drowned on 27 December during severe flooding in an improvised camp for internally displaced people in Sudaniyeh, northwest of Gaza City, the most recent child in Gaza to die due to the extreme winter conditions and lack of safe shelters. At least five other children have lost their lives in December after being exposed to such harsh conditions.

  • General Secretariat condemns Israeli Knesset law to cut electricity and water supplies to UNRWA offices League of Arab States 30 Dec 2025 -- The General Secretariat of the League of Arab States (Palestine and Occupied Arab Territories Sector) condemns the Israeli occupation Knesset's approval of a law mandating cutting electricity and water supplies from the offices of the United Nations Relief and Works Agency for Palestine Refugees in the Near East (UNRWA), a measure that falls within the framework of the sustained targeting of the Agency over recent years.
  • 1210 Israeli Crimes Committed in One Week across Various Palestinian Areas-----The OIC Observatory Records 328 Palestinians killed, 38 Injured, 212 Arrested, and 75 Settler Attacks Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 30 Dec 2025 -- The OIC Media Observatory on Israeli Crimes Against Palestinians recorded 328 Palestinians murdered by Israel and 38 injured across various Palestinian territories between December 23 and 29, 2025
  • Joint Statement on the Gaza Humanitarian Response UK FCDO 30 Dec 2025 -- Joint statement on Gaza from the foreign ministers of Canada, Denmark, Finland, France, Iceland, Japan, Norway, Sweden, Switzerland and the United Kingdom.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Afghanistan to remain major crisis in 2026, UN, partners warn UN News 30 Dec 2025 -- Afghanistan is expected to remain one of the world's largest humanitarian crises in 2026, UN agencies and humanitarian partners warned on Tuesday, launching a $1.7 billion appeal to assist nearly 18 million people in urgent need.

  • Yemeni Government Commends Saudi Historic Stance and Its Leadership of the Coalition to Restore Legitimacy SPA 30 Dec 2025 -- The Yemeni government commended the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's historic and steadfast stances, highlighting its crucial role in ensuring Yemen's security and stability. The government acknowledged Saudi Arabia's leadership of the Coalition to Restore Legitimacy and its ongoing commitment to protecting civilians, de-escalating tensions, and preventing the eastern governorates from being drawn into conflicts that serve hostile agendas
  • Saudi Arabia Expresses Disappointment over Pressure Exerted by UAE on Southern Transitional Council Forces to Conduct Military Operations on Its Southern Borders in Hadramout and Al-Mahara Governorates, Considering It a Threat to National Security SPA 30 Dec 2025 -- The Ministry of Foreign Affairs issued the following statement today:
  • Saudi Arabia Ends Military Operation in Eastern Yemen Sputnik 30 Dec 2025 -- The Saudi-led Arab coalition announced the end of its military operation in the port of Al Mukalla in eastern Yemen after strikes on military cargo destined for the Southern Transitional Council forces.
  • Saudi Arabia Strikes Al Mukalla Port in Eastern Yemen - Reports Sputnik 30 Dec 2025 -- The Saudi-led coalition carried out airstrikes against the port of Al Mukalla in eastern Yemen, targeting military supplies delivered from the United Arab Emirates to separatists from the Southern Transitional Council, Saudi news agency SPA reported on Tuesday, citing the coalition's statement.
  • Aboul Gheit calls for immediate cessation of escalation and prioritising dialogue in Yemen League of Arab States 30 Dec 2025 -- Mr. Ahmed Aboul Gheit, Secretary-General of the League of Arab States, expressed deep concern over the serious and rapidly unfolding developments in the Republic of Yemen, following the failure of the Southern Transitional Council to respond to the demands of the Presidential Leadership Council. He voiced hope for the immediate cessation of escalation in the governorates of Hadramout and Al-Mahra in eastern Yemen.
  • UAE urges 'restraint' after Saudi airstrikes on Yemen's Mukalla port Press TV 30 Dec 2025 -- The United Arab Emirates has called for "restraint and wisdom" following Saudi Arabia's airstrikes on Yemen's port city of Mukalla.

  • Sexual violence against children 'entrenched, systemic and widespread' across DR Congo, UNICEF warns UN News 30 Dec 2025 -- Sexual violence against children has become entrenched, systemic and increasingly widespread across the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), with new data showing a sharp rise in cases since 2022, according to the UN Children's Fund (UNICEF).

  • Sudan: UN warns of unprecedented child hunger in Darfur as fighting fuels refugee exodus UN News 30 Dec 2025 -- Sudan's deepening war is driving unprecedented levels of hunger, child malnutrition and displacement, UN agencies report, as new data from Darfur and a renewed refugee influx into Chad underscore the rapidly deteriorating situation.

  • A3+ condemns Israel's recognition of Somaliland People's Daily 30 Dec 2025 -- The three African members of the Security Council -- Algeria, Sierra Leone, and Somalia -- along with Guyana, collectively known as A3+, on Monday strongly condemned Israel's recognition of Somaliland as a sovereign state.
  • UN Security Council members condemn Israel's 'illegitimate' recognition of Somaliland Press TV 30 Dec 2025 -- The majority of United Nations Security Council member states have condemned Israel's recognition of the breakaway region of Somaliland in the Horn of Africa, with several nations expressing concern that the move could significantly impact the situation of Palestinians in Gaza.

News Reports

  • Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's interview with Rossiya Segodnya International Information Agency, December 30, 2025 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 30 Dec 2025
  • Telephone conversation with President of Uzbekistan Shavkat Mirziyoyev President of Russia 30 Dec 2025
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's interview on the occasion of 15th anniversary of Alexander Gorchakov Fund, Moscow, December 30, 2025 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 30 Dec 2025
  • Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's interview with Rossiya Segodnya International Information Agency, December 30, 2025 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 30 Dec 2025
  • Telephone conversation with President of Kazakhstan Kassym-Jomart Tokayev President of Russia 30 Dec 2025 -- Vladimir Putin had a telephone conversation with President of the Republic of Kazakhstan Kassym-Jomart Tokaye.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Lin Jian's Regular Press Conference on December 30, 2025 PRC MofA 30 Dec 2025
  • Wang Yi: The Relationship Between China and the World is Developing in a More Positive Direction PRC MofA 30 Dec 2025
  • Wang Yi: In 2026, China's Major-Country Diplomacy with Chinese Characteristics Should Accomplish More PRC MofA 30 Dec 2025
  • Wang Yi: Facing A Troubled World Where Peace is Under Threat, China Acts as An Anchor for Stability PRC MofA 30 Dec 2025
  • Wang Yi On Five Contributions of China's Diplomacy in 2025 PRC MofA 30 Dec 2025
  • Wang Yi Attends the Symposium on the International Situation and China's Foreign Relations and Delivers a Keynote Speech PRC MofA 30 Dec 2025
  • Opening up New Horizons for Major-Country Diplomacy with Chinese Characteristics at a Crucial Historical Juncture PRC MofA 30 Dec 2025
  • Wang Yi: China and the U.S. Must Find the Right Way for the Two Major Countries to Get Along with Each Other PRC MofA 30 Dec 2025
  • Wang Yi: Facing Critical Challenges to International Justice, China Acts as A Steadying Force PRC MofA 30 Dec 2025
  • Wang Yi: Facing the Global Development Predicament, China Acts as A Main Engine PRC MofA 30 Dec 2025
  • Wang Yi: Facing A Changing International Order, China Acts as A Defining Force PRC MofA 30 Dec 2025
  • Wang Yi: Facing A New Environment in Its Neighborhood, China Acts as A Pillar of the Region PRC MofA 30 Dec 2025
  • Wang Yi: China Must Resolutely Defend the Victorious Outcomes of World War II Achieved with Great Sacrifice PRC MofA 30 Dec 2025
  • China's home-built AS700 manned airship delivered to buyers after obtaining official mass production license Global Times 30 Dec 2025 -- China's domestically developed AS700 "Xiangyun" manned airship on Tuesday obtained the country's first production certificate (PC) for a homegrown manned airship, marking a major breakthrough in China's low-altitude aviation sector.

  • Federal Foreign Office statement on China's recent military exercises around Taiwan Germany Federal Foreign Office 30 Dec 2025
  • Wang Yi: Any Attempt at Blocking China's Reunification Cause is Doomed to Fail PRC MofA 30 Dec 2025
  • China's Taiwan drills raise global concerns, lawmakers call for action CNA 30 Dec 2025 -- China conducted its second day of military drills around Taiwan, including live-fire exercises, on Tuesday, prompting lawmakers from the United States, Germany and an international organization of legislators, to warn of rising tensions and urge emergency measures to support Taiwan.
  • Trump 'not worried' as China stages live-fire exercises around Taiwan CNA 30 Dec 2025 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said Monday that Chinese President Xi Jinping (習近平) had not informed him in advance of China's ongoing military exercises around Taiwan, adding that he was "not worried" about the operation.
  • Lai says China's drills 'not behavior of a responsible world power' CNA 30 Dec 2025 -- President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) on Tuesday urged unity among Taiwanese as China's latest large-scale military drills around Taiwan entered their second day, accusing Beijing of escalating regional tensions in a manner unbecoming of a responsible world power.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova's comment on the latest threats of military strikes against Iran Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 30 Dec 2025
  • Trump threatens new attack on Iran if it attempts to rearm People's Daily 30 Dec 2025 -- U.S. President Donald Trump on Monday threatened to attack Iran again if Tehran attempts to rearm.
  • Iran responds to Trump's new threats in a letter to UNSC IRNA 30 Dec 2025 -- Iranian Ambassador and Permanent Representative to the UN Amir Saeid Iravani, following US President Donald Trump's new threats against Iran, condemned "these reckless threats" in a letter to the Security Council and the UN Secretary-General.
  • Russia Calls for End to Escalation Around Iran, Its Nuclear Program - Moscow Sputnik 30 Dec 2025 -- Russia is calling on "hotheads" to refrain from escalating tensions around Iran and its nuclear program, Russian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Maria Zakharova said on Tuesday.
  • Putin Holds Phone Conversation With Iranian President - Kremlin Sputnik 30 Dec 2025 -- Russian President Vladimir Putin held on Tuesday a telephone conversation with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian, the Kremlin said.
  • Iranian President Promises Harsh Response to Any Aggression After Trump's Threats Sputnik 30 Dec 2025 -- Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian said on Tuesday, following threats from US President Donald Trump, that Tehran's response to aggression would be harsh.
  • Iran does not fear war; but 'narrow window of opportunity' for dialogue remains open, FM says Press TV 30 Dec 2025 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says Iran does not fear war, but stresses that escalation can still be avoided if concerned parties pursue the available opportunity for dialogue.
  • Iran's foreign minister calls on Trump to defy Netanyahu, revive nuclear talks Press TV 30 Dec 2025 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has urged US President Donald Trump to resist pressure from Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and reopen negotiations with Tehran over its nuclear program, citing growing regional support for diplomacy.
  • Iran's president reacts to Trump's threat, vows 'severe' response to 'oppressive' aggression Press TV 30 Dec 2025 -- Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian has issued a firm rebuttal to Donald Trump's latest threat of renewed strikes against the country, vowing that any further aggression will be met with a crushing and "regret-inducing" response.
  • US issues new sanctions targeting Iran's defense projects Press TV 30 Dec 2025 -- The United States has issued new sanctions targeting eight Iranian individuals and entities over their alleged roles in the country's defense program, including activities related to the development of missiles and unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs).
  • Disrupting Iran's UAV Proliferation to Venezuela and Iran's Weapons Programs US Dept. of State 30 Dec 2025 -- "Today, the United States is sanctioning ten entities and individuals based in Iran and Venezuela"
  • Treasury Targets Iran-Venezuela Weapons Trade US Treasury 30 Dec 2025 -- Today, the U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) is targeting 10 individuals and entities based in Venezuela and Iran, including a Venezuelan company that has contributed to Iran's unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) trade with Venezuela.
  • Iran In 'New Phase Of Turmoil' As Protests Spread Beyond Capital RFE/RL 30 Dec 2025 -- Iran's leadership is facing mounting pressure from abroad and emerging dissent from within as street protests over its reeling economy and the threat of a new round of military strikes hang over the country.
  • Iran: Statement by the High Representative on behalf of the EU on the arrest and detention of human rights defenders EC/CEU 30 Dec 2025 -- The European Union expresses serious concern over the widespread use of arbitrary detention by Iranian authorities to suppress critical voices within the country, in violation of Iran's obligations under the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights.
  • Telephone conversation with President of the Islamic Republic of Iran Masoud Pezeshkian President of Russia 30 Dec 2025
  • Iran says $2bn of frozen assets released by neighboring country Press TV 30 Dec 2025 -- Iran has reportedly been allowed to access around $2 billion of its foreign assets that were frozen due to US sanctions, according to a report published in local media.
  • Iran labels Royal Canadian Navy a 'terrorist organization' in response to IRGC designation Press TV 30 Dec 2025 -- Iran has officially designated the Royal Canadian Navy as a terrorist organization in response to the "illegal decision" by the Canadian government targeting part of the Iranian Armed Forces.
  • Press conference - Canberra Prime Minister of Australia 30 Dec 2025

  • Remarks by Prime Minister Netanyahu and US President Donald Trump after their Meeting at Mar-a-Lago Israel - Prime Minister 30 Dec 2025

  • Khaleda Zia, Bangladesh's first female prime minister, dies at 80 Press TV 30 Dec 2025 -- Begum Khaleda Zia, Bangladesh's first female prime minister and a towering figure in the nation's politics, has passed away at the age of 80.

  • Kosovo's President Says She Expects New Government To Be Formed Quickly RFE/RL 30 Dec 2025 -- Kosovo's President Vjosa Osmani said she expects the country's new parliament and government to be formed quickly following early parliamentary elections, urging lawmakers to immediately prioritize passage of the state budget and ratification of key international agreements.

  • General Assembly approves $3.45 billion UN regular budget for 2026 UN News 30 Dec 2025 -- The General Assembly has approved a $3.45 billion regular budget for the United Nations for 2026, following weeks of intensive negotiations and one of the Organization's most important reform initiatives, UN80.
